Here's what I found about Vitest:

## Overview

Vitest (pronounced as "veetest") is a next generation testing framework powered by Vite. Vitest was created to make testing just work for Vite apps, and by building on top of Vite, Vitest natively understands your Vite config and is able to reuse the same resolve and transform pipelines.

## Key Features

Vitest offers smart & instant watch mode, native code coverage via v8 or istanbul, and Jest-compatible mocking, stubbing, and spies.

Vitest runs tests in parallel across worker threads for maximum speed, and it automatically enables an instant "watch" mode that reruns only the tests related to changed files.

Vitest has first-class support for modern JavaScript out of the box: it handles ESM, TypeScript, and JSX natively via Vite's transformer.

## Advantages

Vitest is Jest-compatible, which means you can reuse much of your existing test suite with minimal changes. Vitest can run tests more than four times faster than Jest in many scenarios.

Unlike Jest, which required Babel integration and TSJest setup, Vitest works out of the box and reuses your existing Vite configuration.

## Current Status

The latest version is 4.1.5, last published 12 days ago.


REMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks.
